steps.title: Identify the relationship between the two sentences
steps.content: Sentence 1: William Still was instrumental in helping nearly 1,000 people escape slavery and earned the title "Father of the Underground Railroad" — he was famous. Sentence 2: "despite the fame of his contributions during his lifetime, Still is discussed far less today" — he is now obscure. The second sentence directly contradicts the first. The transition must signal contrast.

steps.title: Eliminate the wrong choices and confirm B
steps.content:
• Choice A — For example,: Introduces a specific instance of the preceding claim. The second sentence does not give an example of Still's fame; it pivots to his current obscurity. ✗
  • Choice B — However,: Signals a contrast between two ideas. Still was once famous (sentence 1) but is now little discussed (sentence 2) — "however" bridges this opposition exactly. ✓
  • Choice C — Specifically,: Narrows or elaborates on a previous idea. The second sentence does not narrow or elaborate on Still's fame; it contradicts it. ✗
  • Choice D — Similarly,: Signals agreement or parallel between ideas. The two sentences are in opposition, not agreement. ✗
That matches Choice B — However,.
